title: RuntimeIdentity
description: Report-only runtime identity profile for one agent session. Claim ceiling: 'verified' means verified against an approved runtime signal (environment marker, harness-written artifact, or hook payload), NOT cryptographically proven runtime identity; runtime signals can be inherited, overwritten, or forged. Detection failure degrades to 'unknown' with value null — never guessed. Generation must involve no LLM call and no network call.
x_export_prohibited: prompts, conversation content, code, or diffs

detection_tier.description: verified: approved runtime signal (env marker, harness artifact, hook payload). detected: reliable inference (e.g. installed CLI version, config default). reported: agent self-report — never produced by the detector itself. unknown: no signal; value must be null. A reported value must never be recorded as verified.

binding_scope.description: What the signal is actually bound to. session: this specific run (e.g. session-id-keyed transcript). installation: the installed software, not necessarily this run (e.g. CLI --version). config_default: a configured default that this session may have overridden. repo: repository-level fact. Statistics must not treat config_default or installation values as session facts.

signal_source.description: The concrete signal, e.g. 'env:CLAUDECODE' or 'harness_transcript'. Signal names only — never signal values containing paths or secrets.

evidence.description: Short human-readable note. Same export prohibitions apply.

provider.description: e.g. anthropic, openai, google; 'unknown' when undetermined.

family.description: Normalized model family used in the coarse fingerprint, e.g. 'claude-fable', 'gpt-5'. Never string-guessed from an unrecognized id; unrecognized ids map to 'unknown'.

version.description: Version as published by the provider, e.g. '5.6'. Not parsed into 'major' by guessing.

revision.description: Checkpoint/quantization/backend revision when known; 'unknown' otherwise. Aliases may hide backend updates — absence of revision change is not proof of no drift.

raw_id.description: The raw model id string as observed, e.g. 'claude-fable-5'.

detection_status.description: full: agent, model, and surface all verified. partial: at least one identity field above unknown. none: nothing detected; profile is legal but uncalibrated.

surface_class.description: Normalized class used in the coarse fingerprint.

fingerprint_schema_version.description: Version of the fingerprint algorithm itself. A change here means ids are not comparable across versions and must never be interpreted as runtime drift.

coarse_id.description: hash(agent_family + model_family + surface_class). Used for sample accumulation; only a coarse change resets maturity. 'unknown' when agent and model are both undetected.

full_id.description: hash over all known normalized identity fields. Used for drift marking only, never for resetting samples. Must not include usernames, absolute paths, or repo secrets.

coarse_inputs.description: Transparency copy of the coarse fingerprint inputs.

llm_calls.description: Locked to 0: profiles are never LLM-generated.
